title: Handle Gemini API HTTP 429 Without Terminating User Workflow
description: The system shall handle Gemini API HTTP 429 rate-limit responses without terminating the user workflow. The user shall remain able to interact with the application while AI processing is paused for retry.

architectureDecision.principle: Do not call Gemini directly from the React frontend. Route all AI operations through a server-side AI Orchestration Service.
architectureDecision.architecture: React → AI Orchestration API → Queue/Rate Limiter → Gemini
architectureDecision.rationale: This provides a single point to implement retry, backoff, concurrency control, caching, idempotency, circuit breaking, logging, and graceful degradation.

title: Configurable Retry Logic with Exponential Backoff for Retryable AI Failures
description: The system shall implement configurable retry logic with exponential backoff for retryable AI service failures (429, 500, 502, 503, Timeout).

title: Honor Gemini API Retry-After Header When Provided
description: The system shall read and honor the Retry-After header value when included in Gemini API 429 responses. When Retry-After is not provided, fall back to exponential backoff.

title: Configurable Maximum Retry Attempt Limit
description: The system shall limit the maximum number of retry attempts to a configurable value. Do not retry indefinitely. Default: 3 retries.

title: Jitter on Exponential Backoff Intervals
description: The system shall apply random jitter to exponential backoff intervals to reduce synchronized retry behavior when multiple requests are retrying concurrently.

title: Distinguish Retryable from Non-Retryable AI Errors
description: The system shall distinguish retryable errors from non-retryable errors and apply retry logic only to retryable errors.

title: Queue AI Processing Requests at Concurrent Request Limit
description: The system shall queue AI processing requests when concurrent Gemini request limits are reached. Rapid document uploads shall not trigger unlimited concurrent AI calls.
queueBehavior: When queue is full, reject new requests with a user-visible queued state rather than silently dropping them.

title: Document Fingerprinting and Idempotency to Prevent Duplicate AI Processing
description: The system shall prevent duplicate AI processing for the same document through document fingerprinting (SHA-256 hash of file content) and/or idempotency controls using unique AI request IDs.

fingerprintBehavior.onMatch: Return cached extraction result — do not re-call Gemini
fingerprintBehavior.onNoMatch: Call Gemini and cache result keyed by hash

title: Persist Uploaded Documents Before Initiating AI Processing
description: The system shall persist uploaded documents to local storage before initiating AI processing. Document storage shall not be contingent on AI processing success.

title: Preserve Document and Previously Generated Requirements on AI Processing Failure
description: The system shall preserve the uploaded document and all previously generated requirements when AI processing fails. AI processing failure shall not delete or corrupt prior work.

title: User-Accessible Retry Option After AI Processing Failure
description: The system shall provide users with a retry option after AI processing failure. The document and its processing state shall remain accessible with a 'Retry Processing' action.

uiDegradedState.message: AI processing is temporarily unavailable. Your document has been safely saved.

title: User-Friendly Messages for Rate Limits, Timeouts, and AI Service Failures
description: The system shall provide meaningful user-facing messages for rate limits, timeouts, and AI service failures. Raw HTTP status codes (429, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED) shall never be the primary user-facing error message.

userMessages.rateLimit: AI processing is temporarily busy. Your document has been saved and will be retried automatically.
userMessages.permanentFailure: AI processing could not be completed after multiple attempts. Your document and existing requirements are safe. You can retry processing or continue manually.
userMessages.configurationError: The AI service is not configured correctly. Please verify the Gemini API configuration.
userMessages.retrying: AI service is temporarily busy. Retrying automatically... (Attempt 2 of 3, next attempt in 4 seconds)

title: Circuit Breaker for Repeated AI Service Failures
description: The system shall implement circuit-breaker behavior for repeated AI service failures to prevent cascade failures and unnecessary quota consumption.

title: AI Processing Status States: Queued, Processing, Retrying, Completed, Failed, Degraded
description: The system shall maintain explicit AI processing status states and display them to users.

title: Log AI Request IDs, Document IDs, Model, Retry Counts, Response Times, Final Status
description: The system shall log AI request IDs, document IDs, model names, retry counts, response times, token usage, and final processing status for all AI operations.

title: Cached or Pre-Generated AI Results for Critical Demo Scenarios
description: The system shall support cached or pre-generated AI results for critical demo scenarios to allow continued demonstration when the Gemini service is temporarily unavailable.

modes.CACHED_DEMO: Use pre-extracted requirements from cached responses — guaranteed to work
modes.LIVE_AI: Call Gemini in real time — best experience, subject to rate limits

demoModeStrategy.modelFallbackRecommendation: Do not automatically switch between Gemini 2.0 Flash and Gemini 1.5 Pro solely due to 429 unless the fallback has been validated for equivalent output quality. Treat rate limiting as a service-level problem handled by queuing and retries.
